name: box jump down with one leg stabilization
instructions: Stand in front of a box or platform with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and jump onto the box, landing softly with one foot on the box and the other foot hanging off the edge.
Stabilize yourself on the box with the foot that is on it, while keeping the other foot off the ground.
Hold this position for a few seconds, engaging your calf muscles to maintain balance.
Slowly step down with the foot that is on the box, returning to the starting position.
Repeat the exercise with the other leg.

Stand in front of a box or platform with your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees and jump onto the box, landing softly with one foot on the box and the other foot hanging off the edge. Stabilize yourself on the box with the foot that is on it, while keeping the other foot off the ground. Hold this position for a few seconds, engaging your calf muscles to maintain balance. Slowly step down with the foot that is on the box, returning to the starting position. Repeat the exercise with the other leg.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Single-leg or balance-intensive variations increase fall risk. Use support or a seated alternative.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
